papers.title: Effect of annealing temperature on the microstructure and tensile properties of Fe–10Mn–10Al–0.7C low-density steel
papers.paper_scope: Investigate microstructure evolution and tensile properties of Fe-10Mn-10Al-0.7C low-density steel after annealing at 700-1200°C for 1 h, focusing on κ-carbide dissolution, grain growth, and their effects on strength and ductility.

alloys.alloys_notes: Measured composition: 10.4 wt.% Mn, 9.7 wt.% Al, 0.70 wt.% C, Fe balance. Density about 6.80 g/cm³, approximately 13% lower than pure iron. Designed as a Mn-lean low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steel.

processes.description: Hot-rolled state: vacuum induction melting, forging, homogenization at 1200°C for 1 h, hot rolling to 3 mm with finishing around 880°C, water quenched to about 300°C.
processes.processes_notes: Shared prior thermomechanical processing for all samples.

processes.description: Annealed at 700°C for 1 h after hot rolling, followed by water quenching.

Annealed at 1000°C for 1 h after hot rolling, followed by water quenching.
Annealed at 1100°C for 1 h after hot rolling, followed by water quenching.
Annealed at 1200°C for 1 h after hot rolling, followed by water quenching.

structures.overall_structure: Banded structure with large α phase areas and γ phase areas; inhomogeneous microstructure; coarse ferrite from early solidification observed.

phases_present.morphology: Large grains in banded structure, Al-rich
Continuous γ phase areas, Mn-rich and C-rich
Along α/γ and α/α boundaries by SEM

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Inhomogeneous; coarse ferrite grains formed during early solidification

structures.overall_structure: Fully eutectoid microstructure: γ almost totally decomposed to lamellar α + κ; continuous κ-carbide along large α grain boundaries; yield phenomenon observed.

phases_present.morphology: Large grains with lamellar α+κ regions
Lamellar within α matrix and continuous along α grain boundaries

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Large α grains containing lamellar κ-carbide

precipitates.distribution: Lamellar within α matrix and continuous along prior γ grain boundaries
precipitates.coherency: Nishiyama-Wasserman orientation relationship with α matrix in lamellar regions; some globular/short-rod κ incoherent

structures.overall_structure: α + γ + κ-carbide; banded structure remains; κ-carbide mainly between α/γ and along α/α boundaries, some dispersed in γ area; κ coarsened and closer to globular.

phases_present.morphology: Long strip α phase in banded structure
γ phase areas with some α or κ dispersed
Globular or short-rod like; mainly at α/γ and α/α boundaries; some in γ area

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Banded structure with long strip α phase

precipitates.distribution: At α/γ and α/α boundaries; some in γ area
precipitates.coherency: No orientation relationship found between κ-carbide and neighboring α phase

structures.overall_structure: α + γ + κ-carbide; γ fraction increased to about 39%; κ in γ area disappeared; κ along α/α boundaries still exists without much change.

phases_present.morphology: Banded α phase
γ phase areas increased
Reduced near γ phase; continuous along α/α boundaries

precipitates.distribution: Continuous along α/α boundaries; reduced near γ phase areas

structures.overall_structure: α + γ duplex microstructure; no obvious κ-carbide in matrix; banded structure less contiguous; boundary κ-carbide formed during quenching along some large α/α boundaries.

phases_present.morphology: α₁ grains among γ grains and α₂ banded ferrite
Continuous γ matrix
Boundary κ-carbide along some large α/α boundaries formed during quenching

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: α₁ (among γ grains) ~3.5 μm; γ ~5.0 μm; α₂ (banded) ~26.0 μm

structures.overall_structure: α + γ duplex microstructure; grain sizes increased compared to 900°C; no obvious matrix κ-carbide; boundary κ-carbide present.

phases_present.morphology: α₁ among γ grains; α₂ banded
γ matrix

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: α₁ ~3.0 μm; γ ~8.5 μm; α₂ ~30.0 μm

structures.overall_structure: α + γ duplex microstructure; γ fraction largest (~63%); grains grow apparently; banded structure less contiguous.

phases_present.morphology: α₁ among γ grains; α₂ banded
γ matrix

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: α₁ ~4.5 μm; γ ~13.0 μm; α₂ ~41.0 μm

structures.overall_structure: α + γ duplex microstructure; grain sizes increased significantly; no obvious matrix κ-carbide; boundary κ-carbide present.

phases_present.morphology: α₁ among γ grains; α₂ banded
γ matrix

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: α₁ ~7.5 μm; γ ~22.0 μm; α₂ ~47.0 μm

structures.overall_structure: α + γ duplex microstructure with very large grains; needle-like or plate-like FCC particles in some large α grains; boundary κ-carbide along α/α boundaries.
structures.number_of_phases: 2 (α and γ); trace boundary κ-carbide; FCC particles

phases_present.morphology: Very large α grains; α₂ banded less contiguous
Large γ grains

precipitates.coherency: Nishiyama-Wasserman orientation relationship with α matrix; large misorientation between neighboring κ particles

phases.coherence: Possibly coherent or low fraction (not detected by XRD, observed along boundaries by SEM)
Nishiyama-Wasserman orientation relationship in lamellar regions

interfaces.phase_evolution: κ-carbide coarsening destroys coherency and orientation relationship with α matrix as annealing temperature increases.
Boundary κ-carbide forms along α/α boundaries during quenching from temperatures above dissolution temperature.

characterization_methods.technique: X-ray diffraction (XRD, Rigaku D/Max-RB, Cu target, 40 kV, 150 mA)
characterization_methods.purpose: Phase identification and volume fraction determination
characterization_methods.key_findings: Identified α, γ, and κ-carbide phases in hot-rolled and annealed specimens; volume fractions determined by direct comparison method; lattice parameters of α (2.89–2.91 Å), γ (3.65–3.67 Å), and κ-carbide (3.76–3.79 Å) determined.
Banded structure observed in RD-ND and RD-TD planes; large α grains and γ areas; inhomogeneous microstructure.

characterization_methods.technique: Field-emission scanning electron microscopy (FE-SEM, FEI Quanta 450, 20 kV)
characterization_methods.key_findings: κ-carbide along α/γ and α/α boundaries in HR specimen; secondary cracks and fracture modes (ductile and quasi-cleavage) in tensile specimens observed at 800, 900, and 1000°C.
Lamellar κ-carbide and α matrix with Nishiyama-Wasserman orientation relationship; (200)κ dark-field image from [100]κ direction; some globular or short-rod like κ without orientation relationship.
No orientation relationship found between κ-carbide and neighboring α phase; SADPs of α, γ, and κ phases obtained.
Boundary κ-carbide along α/α boundaries confirmed by BF micrograph and SADP.
Boundary κ-carbide with N-W orientation relationship to α matrix; large misorientation between neighboring κ particles; needle-like or plate-like FCC particles in large α grains.

characterization_methods.technique: SEM equipped with energy dispersive spectrometer (EDS)
characterization_methods.key_findings: Mn higher and Al lower in α phase compared with γ phase; Mn and Al contents highest in κ-carbide.
Elemental concentration profiles consistent with 800°C specimen; κ-carbide enriched in Mn and Al.
UTS 855 MPa, YS 651 MPa, elongation 36.6%, product of strength and ductility 31 GPa%; continuous yielding; tensile axis parallel to rolling direction at initial strain rate 3×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹.
Density about 6.80 g/cm³, approximately 13% lower than pure iron.

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated phase fractions and eutectoid temperature (~823°C) compared with measured values; actual κ-carbide dissolution temperature (850–900°C) is higher than calculated, attributed to non-equilibrium conditions and thermodynamic calculation error.

unmapped_findings: The lattice parameters determined from XRD profiles are: α phase 2.89–2.91 Å, γ phase 3.65–3.67 Å, κ-carbide 3.76–3.79 Å.

Fig. 16 caption table provides tensile data for the hot-rolled (HR) specimen (UTS ~1100 MPa, YS ~1000 MPa, elongation ~62%, PSE ~12 GPa%), which is absent in the main text Table 1 and not explicitly discussed as a measured data point in the body.

Fig. 15 shows engineering stress–strain curves and true stress–true strain curves with work hardening rate curves; quantitative values are not tabulated in the text, and trends are described qualitatively (e.g., work hardening rates at true strain <5% are in reverse order of annealing temperatures for 700–850°C specimens, and lower for 900–1200°C specimens).
No TRIP or TWIP phenomenon was observed during tensile testing based on post-deformation TEM examination (images not shown).
The κ-carbide dissolution temperature measured (850–900°C) is higher than the Thermo-Calc equilibrium calculation (~823°C), which is attributed to non-equilibrium conditions and calculation error.
In the 1200°C specimen, needle-like or plate-like FCC particles formed in some large α grains during quenching, competing with boundary κ-carbide for Mn and C.

Fracture mechanisms: 800°C specimen shows mixture of ductile fracture (γ phase) and quasi-cleavage fracture (α phase); 900°C and 1000°C specimens show predominant mixture of ductile γ and quasi-cleavage α, with 900°C having more obvious ductile area; large secondary cracks related to banded structure observed in all fractographs.
Grain size measurement method: SEM micrographs analyzed with ImageJ software; α ferrite classified into α₁ (mainly among γ grains) and α₂ (banded, large grains) due to large size difference.
